senses.definition: any form of media content that is stored, transmitted, and accessed in digital format, encompassing various types of multimedia content such as text, images, audio, and video
digital content, such as articles, videos, images, and audio, that is published and accessed through Internet-based platforms and websites
websites and applications enabling users to share content and build communities on their smartphones, computers, etc.

examples.x: She spends hours on social media connecting with friends.
He used social media to promote his new business.
They shared photos and updates on social media during their trip.
He is concerned about privacy issues related to social media.
They discussed the impact of social media on society.

senses.definition: the real-time delivery of audio and video content over the Internet, allowing users to access and enjoy the content without the need for downloading

examples.x: I spent the afternoon watching movies on a streaming media service like Netflix.
She listens to music all day long using a streaming media app on her phone.
The streaming media service froze during the live broadcast of the game.
He prefers streaming media because he can watch anything anytime without waiting for a download.
Streaming media has made it easy to find almost any song or movie within seconds.

senses.definition: the distribution of audio and video content, such as television and radio programs, to a wide audience through over-the-air transmission or cable or satellite networks, providing mass communication and entertainment
the traditional form of mass communication, such as newspapers, magazines, books, and other printed materials, that convey information and entertainment through ink and paper

examples.x: Many people still prefer print media for getting in-depth articles and features.
Print media has been around for centuries, long before the rise of the Internet.
The company decided to advertise its new product in print media and on social media.
Some people argue that print media is more reliable than online sources.

senses.definition: the collective body of established and widely recognized media outlets that cater to a broad audience, shaping public opinion and providing news, information, and entertainment within the boundaries of conventional norms

examples.x: The story was all over the mainstream media, so everyone heard about it.
The mainstream media has been criticized for focusing too much on celebrity gossip.
The influence of mainstream media can shape how people view important issues.
Mainstream media is slowly losing its audience as people turn to online platforms.
The coverage of the election by mainstream media seemed to focus on drama rather than policy.

senses.definition: different sources of information and communication that offer alternative views, question what is commonly believed, and focus on voices not usually heard in mainstream media

examples.x: Alternative media often provides a space for marginalized communities to express themselves outside of traditional media channels.
Independent journalists often rely on alternative media platforms to share stories that mainstream outlets ignore.
Alternative media can be a great way to learn about topics that the mainstream news might not cover in depth.
Many people turn to alternative media to get news from sources that are not influenced by large corporations.

senses.definition: media outlets, such as television stations, radio stations, newspapers, or online platforms, that are owned, controlled, or heavily influenced by the government or state authorities

examples.x: In some countries, state media is the only source of news available to the public.
The government's announcements were covered extensively by the state media.
State media often reflect the viewpoints of the ruling political party.
Critics argue that state media can be biased and only show one side of the story.
Journalists working for state media are often expected to align with government policies.

senses.definition: widespread forms of communication, including TV, radio, newspapers, magazines, and the Internet, that reach a large audience with news, information, and entertainment

examples.x: Mass media plays a big role in shaping how people view world events.
News channels are a type of mass media that provide information to a large audience.
The rise of social media has changed how mass media reaches people.
Advertisers use mass media to promote their products to as many people as possible.

senses.definition: digital content or platforms that enable active user participation and engagement through interactive features and two-way communication

examples.x: The app uses interactive media to guide users through step-by-step instructions for cooking a meal.
Many educational apps use interactive media to help students learn in a fun and engaging way.
Interactive media makes online quizzes more enjoyable, as you can instantly see your results after completing them.
Video games are a popular form of interactive media where players can make decisions that affect the outcome.

senses.definition: advertisements or promotional messages displayed in public spaces, such as billboards and digital screens, targeting audiences outside of their homes or workplaces

examples.x: The city council put up outdoor media to inform the public about the new recycling program.
I saw an outdoor media ad for a concert while walking to the train station.
Companies often use outdoor media to promote their brand during peak shopping seasons.
The new movie was advertised on outdoor media, with giant billboards all over the city.

senses.definition: the activity or profession of making commercial announcements to promote a product or service

examples.x: Successful advertising helps brands attract more customers.
Without effective advertising, new products struggle to gain recognition.
Social media has changed the landscape of modern advertising.

senses.definition: the profession of collecting and editing pieces of news and articles either to be published in a newspaper, magazine, etc. or broadcast

examples.x: She studied journalism to become a reporter.
Journalism plays a crucial role in keeping the public informed.
The course covers various aspects of journalism, including writing and ethics.
He pursued a career in journalism after graduating from college.
